nativeRange.habitat: Flooded rice paddies, shallow wetlands, and marshy lowlands

decorPreferences: open background area for stem growth

summary: A vibrant Southeast Asian stem plant that develops red-pink coloration under bright light and iron-rich conditions; well suited as a midground-to-background accent in planted aquariums.
careNotes: Limnophila aromatica is a stem plant native to the rice paddies of Southeast Asia, where it grows both as a wild plant and as a cultivated culinary herb (Vietnamese ngò ôm, used in canh chua and similar dishes; also known in Thai cuisine as phak khaeyng). In aquariums it is generally considered intermediate-difficulty: it tends to grow well in any planted tank, but the prized red-pink leaf coloration only develops reliably with a combination of high lighting, CO2 injection, and an iron-rich (nutrient-rich) substrate. Under lower light and no CO2 it still grows but tends to stay green with only purple leaf undersides. Sources broadly agree on a 21–28 °C temperature range, pH 6.0–7.5, and soft-to-moderately-hard water (gH ~3–14, kH ~2–8). Adult submerged height has been reported between 20 and 50 cm, with most aquarium specimens settling around 30–40 cm; trim regularly to keep the lower stems from going leggy and to encourage lateral bushing. Substrate should be fine gravel or aqua soil supplemented with root tabs for best growth. The 'Vietnamese Coriander' trade name is a common misnomer — Limnophila aromatica is not related to true Vietnamese coriander (Persicaria odorata), but the trade label is widely used. Taxonomy note: most current treatments place this species in Plantaginaceae (older sources sometimes list Scrophulariaceae).
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings — snip a 7–10 cm length from a healthy stem (ideally including a node), strip the lowest leaves, and replant directly into the substrate. The plant also produces lateral shoots from older stems that can be separated and replanted. Emersed growth produces small pink-to-purple flowers and can set seed, though seed propagation is rare in aquarium settings.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Primary product page provided height, light/CO2 guidance, propagation, family taxonomy, and red-color iron note. Tropica plant detail page (4473) was the manifest-supplied primary source but rendered an XSLT error / blank content on multiple WebFetch attempts; Buce Plant was promoted to primary, and the playbook fallback was followed.

additional.name: aqua-fish.net (Limnophila aromatica care guide)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (21–28 °C), pH (6–7.5), hardness (3–14 °dGH), height (30–40 cm), light demand (bright), propagation, native range, and culinary use. Used as the primary secondary because the manifest-designated Tropica page could not be retrieved.

additional.name: Aqualand Pets Plus (Limnophila aromatica care guide)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (22–28 °C), pH (5.0–7.5 across two ranges), gH 4–8 / kH 3–6, height (20–40 cm), light, growth rate, propagation, substrate, and iron requirement for red coloration.

additional.name: Wikipedia (Limnophila aromatica)
additional.notes: Confirmed family Plantaginaceae (order Lamiales), native range, scientific synonyms (Limnophila aromaticoides, L. chinensis subsp. aromatica), and Vietnamese/Cambodian/Thai vernacular names plus culinary context. Used for taxonomic anchoring only.
